text: Beers are mediocre at best, food is no great shakes, and we left it at that, signed our debit card slip and called it a night. Next day we received a message, for duplicate charges from our bank. Got to the bank and got the passbook updated with the duplicate charges clearly detailed. Alarmed with this, we reached Biere Club at 11am, spoke to staff on duty, then a couple of managers, and after a frustrating half hour finally a Mr Vijay Kumar, who announced he was the owner and we will have to follow their procedure for the refund, which meant accompanying 2 clueless staff members to the HDFC branch on MG road, 12.40pm at the bank the manager could not find duplicate credits on their...read more

Me and my friends had heard alot about this place. So we thought of going to this new place. Its right at the entrance of Lavelle road opposite to Sunny's or the Mocha road. If its a weekend (or when I went, even a weekday), make sure you reserve your table prior, if you don't want to end up spending your time in front of the bartender. There's a floor where smoking is allowed as well. The place is very lively and has an Irish pub theme. The menu when I visited was limited. They had Lager, Stout, Ale and Wheat. Stout was the special thing for everyone here. These people have their very own micro brewery, which is visible to all from inside. Their beer tastes unique and good, the Stout...read more

Last week a bunch of friends and I figured we'd hit Hard Rock Cafe since it'd been a while we'd grabbed a beer. Turned out we couldn't get ourselves in. Patience is not something we guys take pride in. So we leave and just as we get closer to UB City, we see a new place right opposite the restaurant Sunny's on Lavelle road. It looked pretty cool from outside so we decide to give it a try. It was called The Biere Club. Three floors of awesomeness. We picked the third, smoking area. Done up pretty well, this pub. Very Irish. To our surprise they apparently had their own micro-brewery. I made up my mind to try out every type they had but unfortunately, they had only Lager, Ale and Stout. But...read more
If u are beer crazy n wanna try new flavours of beer - Must go place!!

Summary: A microbrewery which churns decent beer. Not recommended on a weekend night, unless prepared for a long wait. Maybe good for a larger party, and definitely not the place for a quiet drink. The Biere club is the city's first (and only?) microbrewery, I am told. No wonder everyone wants to try it out. We landed there on a Saturday evening at around 9pm. The place was quite chaotic -- all the tables were occupied, and there was no one who could be contacted to know about the wait situation. We ended up standing most of the evening and finally got a table at around 10:15pm. The place offers four kinds of beers - stout, lager, ale and wheat. They haven't given names to these, which...read more

Micro-breweries are the essence of what brewing a beer stands for, innovation, robust flavor and a personal touch. Unfortunately, in India, we are rarely given the opportunity to experience the goodness the comes out of the experimentation that micro-breweries around the world are afforded to make. The Biere Club, by advertising itself as Bangalore's first micro-brewery, has tried to put an end to that and the lack of originality that is seeping through Â the "pub capital of India" when it comes to beer, and, boy, did they knock it out off the park.Â The Biere Club, located at the intersection of Lavelle Road and Vittal Mallya Road, is three floors of beery goodness.Â The ambience...read more
Lovely Beer. Good selection. Nice staff. Very nice casual ambience. Wish their chairs were a little more comfortable!

Call it the 'Leopold Cafe' of Bengaluru or a 'I can do better than you Koshys!' kind of place, The Biere Club deserves a thumbs up - for their delicious beers, the ambience and the guts to bring up a micro-brewery right smack in Mallaya territory. Be that as it may, my friend (a reluctant Banglorian, who is non-trusting of anything 'new' in Bengaluru) and I enjoyed a lovely afternoon sipping our Golden Ale with the light drizzle in the backdrop offering a great ambience. Sometimes you want to go to a place, catch up over a drink and have a conversation. A kind where you can hear each other without screaming yourself hoarse and silly. The Biere Club allowed us this and more, with the...read more

I had been to the Biere Club recently with a friend and trust me, the experience was MIND-BLOWING! Belgian Ale was on top of your minds with the highest alcohol content (9%). We had the Mixed Platter which was served with really tasty grub. Hospitality was really nice. We were a bit late for our reservation and they called us up and confirmed. The waiters were courteous and kept coming back to ask whether we were enjoying the experience or not. The only drawback to Biere Club are the prices. But, to maintain such a place, I bet it costs a pretty dime. But, rest assured, this is the place to go if you want to have a killer time with your friends. Biere Club :)

Beer lovers this is "THE" place to go. Amazing place that serves a huge variety of beer. The music is great and so is the ambience. There are 3 floors in all, the top most being an open air,smoking zone. The place is quite crowded during the weekends so be sure to get your reservations early. We did not explore the food there a lot but among starters the platters can be a good bet for both veg and non-veg. The in-house DJ plays some great music and the crowd is one of the best in the city.Once you go there prepared to get hooked on. Thumbs up!
Superb place to unwind with top quality beers from around the world and home made beers. One of the best places in bangalore to unwind with BEER. One word - superb.

Wanna drink great beer and chill out after a busy day. Then come to The Biere Club! There is nothing like beer brewed in the same building you are drinking in. The food menu is diverse and since I'm partial to Mediterranean food, I was in heaven. There is a great crowd, warm ambiance, and affordable beer, so there is nothing more to ask for! Cheers!

You had better top up your pockets before you enter,cause the prices here are not on the lower side. Having said that ,every penny spent will be worth it if you crave for something different than what the city has to offer. I would insist you try all the samplers for the different varieties of biere on offer. Seasonal offer is a Mango Beer. Says it all really, can't think of another pub in bangalore serving up such concoctions other than Monkey Bar. Soak in the ambiance for all it's worth and sit back and enjoy. Food is very soulful, as in it's not dripping in spices, and does not negate the taste of your drink. Location at the center of the City makes it all the more...read more

Decent microbrewery Pretty decent place in B'lore. Very spacious, and nice ambience in general. Located bang opposite UB CIty. I had their seasonal beer (mango), which I found to be quite good. On the downside, the staff is not very prompt in their service.

Good beer... Had the Wheat beer , tasted better than the wheat beer at TOIT, staff also better and more polite. Food was also good , good presentation. Good woodwork inside , interiors not as good as TOIT, but casual beer drinking environment. Overall a good place to get beer drunk.

Awesome and huge place with friendly staff, great drinks and a vast array of food items to choose from. Though I never tried the "bierre" as I am not a beer person but the martinis and cocktails were good. The music is decent as well. A must place to go for all the beer lovers!

Amazing place to go to. The beer is just fabulous and you can really taste the difference between your normal kingfisher, bud etc. The food is scrumptious. We had ordered the crunchy chicken, sausages wrapped in bacon and prawns, each dish was cooked to perfection and all were equally mouth watering!! And the Star of the day was the stand-up comedian Saurabh Pant who was completely hilarious. I hope they have more stand up comedians come there!!! I will definitely go. Overall a fantastic experience! I highly recommend !!!

We went to the Biere Club on a sunday afternoon last month.. I love the location of Biere Club and absolutely loved the feel of the place.. (way better than TOIT, if you ask me!) The ground floor was almost full.. but we got a table immediately.. Had a Spanish Omlette and a sandwich..can't remember which.. both quite good.. Coming to the beer.. I found it just about ok.. nothing to rave about!
THE BIERE CLUB!!, bangalore deserves this one!. This is just the place when you and your friends can sit-stand-lean drink, talk, laugh your lungs out, sing along and get drunk. We love this place.

Having been thwarted once on a 'table for 2 after 7pm on Saturday' at TOIT, we decided to try another tack at The Biere Club and reserved a table via Poshvine for Sunday noon. We sat on the ground floor, with a view of Lavelle Road as well as the microbrewery. There are two more floors - the smoking sections, I think. Lots of graphics on the walls, reminded me a bit of Mario Miranda. Looks like a peaceful place, without the buzz that TOIT seems to have, but that was probably because of the time we were there at. From the huge selection of beverages and meatables, (don't worry, there are veg options in plenty too) we decided to go for a wheat beer (you get a 500 ml for the price of 330...read more

The whole concept of the micro breweries are simply mind blowing. Biere Club stands up to the mark. The wheat beer here is a big favourite. The staff is very friendly and polite. Although, the food portions could be a little bigger for the price.
The city's first microbrewery, Biere Club is spread across three floors, serves six kinds of home brewed beer. Good Mediterranean food and great music add to the ambience.

I love this place, I wish I lived closer so I could go there more often, decor is beautiful, cozy, the most friendly staff I ever met, strawberry margaritas are to die for, beer is also amazing, if you don't know what to choose you can always order beer samples, food is very tasty, maybe bit expensive but its worth it, great place to chill

I would like to share a funny thing that took place at the Biere Club. During my recent visit which was a saturday evening, there was a huge crowd and all the tables were reserved. I didn't care much about finding a seat, so stood there with my favorite - the STOUT. Then this tall guy (foreign national) walks in and asks for a table for 5 to a staff to which he politely replied "Sir, all tables are fully reserved, you may have to wait". Then this guy goes on to say " I will pay you a 500/- Rs. tip and ensure the final bill tonight wont be less than 20,000/-". And guess what, the staff didn't buy it, the guy had to wait some more time. I was awestruck! Good to know that the staff is...read more
Fantastic place, Awesome beer with some great food. I sure will keep coming back for more! Any plans to open up a place in south Bangalore? ;)

Went to Biere club with a huge group of friends. We had reserved the first floor seating as that provides a more restaurant like seating as opposed to the ground floor which is more like a bar (but I guess that's the point). None of us being beer drinkers and most of us vegetarians, we had an awesome time. The food was really good, and so were the cocktails, the Pom basil martini was absolutely delish. But I suppose for my group of friends who don't drink beer and aren't about to be converted, we kind of missed the point. But that's ok. It was AWESOME fun!! Thanks. The only comment was that even for a group of girls, the portions were really measly. The bread platter was dry, old bread...read more
superbbbbbbbb bear.......... nice ambiance, lovely people, excellent service.....

Love the high-backed sofa-chairs on the top floor (what are they called?). Perfect to unwind on friday evenings with a small group. Big fan of the cocktails and the drinking platters. The place to celebrate match victories with your team and cheering squad. :)

Biere Club is one of my favourite 'beer' hangouts in the city. I usually am loyal to my Wheat Biere here and it's almost always a favourite for most people I know - the ones who prefer their beer light, minus the bitter taste. The Stout Biere here is another gem. I'm usually not the biggest fan of the food here. I think their Hummus and Falafel and some other Mediterranean food is alright, but their platters and other bites don't come off as particular 'tasty' food. It's okay and there is some flavour but it lacks zing. It's not something I particularly enjoy paying for. Which is why when they opened their Chophouse, I was mighty excited. I like good food with my alcohol and it was...read more

This is the best place for having the In House brew style beer (microbrewery), love the Stout, Wheat and Dark Ale. A must visit for people who love to try different types and flavour of beer. Try their new Chilli Lager its different and great. Also there are many restaurants trying to open something similar, but let me tell you one thing they are NO WHERE CLOSE!! Biere Club stands out food wise too, they have excellent presentation and the food is absolutely finger licking good. I simply cannot get enough of this place. Service may be slow at times but its understandable. Highly Recommended: * Olive Dash, Bacon wrapped sausages (blissful), Assorted Crostini, Salads are great and so...read more

The Good: Top floor is open on two sides and the tables by the side give you a nice view of the road where you can indulge in some people-watching. And yes - you can smoke! The music is soft (maybe a bit too soft as they don't turn up the volume when the place fills up). The beer is reasonably priced but isn't anything that I'd rave about. But it would at least satisfy my craving for microbrew ale in b'lore. All beers are far better than at Toit. But these guys didn't have their stout on the day I went. I did a beer flight and then decided that none of the beers stood out particularly. I prefer hoppier ales. But these beers were all pretty all right. Nothing that anyone could take...read more
Kool place to hangout with friends. Food, drink n crowd were awesome.
A meat place that does meat well. Good beer. Vegetarians will feel let down here. And apart from the meat and beer, the chi-chi atmosphere and people-watching are basically the other attractions.
Good place and good food however the portion size could be a little bigger for the price they charge. The place was full and service was a bit slow, they should hire more staff.

The beer is just great at this place.. The decor of this place has graffiti on its walls and the place is tightly packed all the time and I always end up standing and drinking. I think i should start thinking about reserving a place here. They have a smoking section which is on the 1st floor. I didnt get a a chance to sit there but i am sure it would be equally good at the top as well. Many times you end up seeing a lot of famous page 3 personalities here partying on a saturday night. I have been lucky at that. A well deserved place when you wanna eat and drink beer. They serve different varieties of beer and ensure that you try them out in small glasses which you could get if you wish...read more

Beers and Pizzas. Though these two words aren't enough to describe this place, still they make the most of what this place is. We went in late despite reservation and after a bit of argument with the manager happened to the get the seat. Knew this place was famous for beers and ordered ale. My oh my, it was a great delight, guzzled a litre in no time. Raked in some other stuff from my friend's mugs too German Pilsner: Decent Wheat-beer: Good Ale: Awesome Pizzas we ordered: 1) Sun-dried, mushroom, tomato and goat-cheese - Awesome, awesome pizza, tasted just perfect with the beer and otherwise too 2) Charred sweet peppers and smoked cheese - a bit of let down, but okay Ambiance - 7/10...read more

Very friendly staff... Good beer selection too. My friend and I once went when there was a stand-up comedy show (coincidence). We just wanted to catch up and have beer and not really interested in any shows. We were allowed but only at the bar counter... No tables, so it was not really comfortable. We had one round of beer and went to some other place. So please call to find out and book a table is what I can suggest.
Food is good however the service was very delayed. Went there with my friends and had to wait half an hour each order.

From the UK, so I do enjoy a good pint. that being said, its rare to find a microbrewery that has anything that tastes remotely better than a watered down blonde ale. prices are UK prices, but the house beer was better than most. Watch out for your bill to get out of control if you go with a group. will be back

This is one happening place on lavelle road... beautifully made.. and it is packed everytime you think of going there.. so better book well in advance.. go there if ur a beer lover and wanna experiment on various flavours!! the food is nice too.. A must visiti!!

The beer whatever you want is never there ,the food is tasty however took nearly 45 mins to get to us . maybe all brewerys have the same problem. the staff like the other brewery is arrogant as well Value: 5/10 Food : 8/10 Ambience : 4/10 Service: 3/10

Biere Club opened up as the city's first microbrewery. Located in a part of town that's known to be the center stage for all the Page 3 and the usual hoity-toity types, The Biere Club is a class above the other micro breweries in town at this point in time. While Toit (the nearest competition) caters to a different age segment, The Biere Club is a classier act. Done up like a typical kicked back place with seating across 2 floor (with their newly opened restaurant Chop House in between), they also have a graffiti wall, complete with Mario Miranda cartoons. The atmosphere is completely laid back, and there's a sense of calm even in the chaotic madness that can ensue, especially during...read more

This place is perfect in terms of variety of food, beer and the ambience. Just go there and chill. The beers brewed there are really good and offer a good alternative to the regular stuff available elsewhere.

No Matter you are here on a Saturday Afternoon or a Monday evening, you will find this place flooded with beer lovers loitering around the place with a mug of fresh beer in their hand and grooving to the music. This Place serves the best beer in the city. Not to forget the good food they serve. If you are in Bangalore..this place is a MUST VISIT !!
The food is great, the beer is good third best beer in town however the best food of all the breweries.

I loved the Mario Miranda style on the extreme right wall of the ground floor. I suspect it may be a creation of Paul Fernandes. The black sofas are super-comfortable. Like another brewery in the city, their samplers come at a cost! That, I found a little surprising. A suggestion - make the test tube a smaller shot glass and provide a free sample to each table. Goodwill takes you a longer way than does immediate profit! Anyhow, the German Pilsner was the tastiest one to me and we couldn't resist calling for it mug after mug! On the other hand, I couldn't get myself to like the burnt and strong after taste of the Stout and the Indian Ale. (I do hope I am getting the names right). The...read more

Its really about beer - all beer I have had over the last few months have been good. Recommend try the sampler first and then pick the right one if you plan to have 2-3 beers in the evening. The starter platter is excellent and quite filling especially for a long evening with > 5 drinks. The meats are really fine and found them excellent with ales, stouts and other darker beers. Atmosphere is good, service fast and great ambience. Walls painted like you will see at Leopold in Mumbai.

The bier club..a nice pub located near UB city...The moment you will enter the pub,you will start feeling good about you,about the pub and about everything around.It has a good feel in it. The music they play is awesome..the pictures on the wall add to its charm. There are micro breweries which brew beer and they serve fresh beer..the taste of beer is also different and nice The Foodliquor menu has a wide variety of options with food items you will hardly find anywhere else in Bangalore. Downside- 1)Its full during most of the weekends.So make prior booking or be ready to stand near the bar. 2)Wheat beer is not available more often than not.

Been to Biere club a number of times and have never been disappointed. Their self brewed beers are really good. The place has 3 floors in total. The ground floor which is very much like an old pub with nice bar stools and spacious table arrangements. The 1st floor is now called the Chophouse specializing in Steak. The 2nd floor is the open rooftop where you can smoke and enjoy your drinks. I always go for the Weiss beer which is the strongest of the lot and its good. Their snacks like the potato wedges are real good. The have an option of cooking the wedges with different variety of sauces which is a great hit among me and my friends. The pizza are really yum as well. The chicken wings...read more

This place easily serves one of the best beers in the city. I tried the nonveg platter along with it and it was heavenly. It has really awesome food and trust me its goes great with great beer. Must try place for beer and food lovers..

This was the first microbrewery I had visited and I was quite disappointed with the beer. Not much differentiation between the beers in terms of flavors etc. But the rest of the bar menu was good and made up for it. But whats worth mentioning is the food menu both in terms of quantity and quality it was an amazing feast. Would recommend for a Sunday afternoon chilling...the ambiance is good and when the place fills up it gives a old world charm with its wooden flooring and decor. Loved the overall experience.

Most amazing place for Beer lovers in Bangalore. They serve Best beers in the city, and with amazing food...... Manager and employees are really good and helpful, though last time, I felt one or two of them were not behaving properly (to someone sitting next to my table), may be they were new to Biere Club, and need time to adapt to the Club...... Love the way, you guys have divided all three floors, with different ambience. But, last time when i went to Biere Club, was little disappointed to see that 1st floor was converted into Fine Dining, ealier it was better...... I so miss Biere Club here in Mumbai..... Keep up the good work guys !

Decent Stout served at this place, good for evening gathering with office colleagues or Friday night with Friends. Delicious apetizers and one place that actually has fresh Mozarella in their bruschetta. You will have a nice evening.

Service Once the order is placed, it will be there at your table before you know it. The music and the crowd is simply too good, so you won't actually mind the delay in placing the order. Drinks The beer is really fresh, true to its name and nature. And it goes so well with the music. I personally loved the german lager. But the club special is the one if you like a strong aroma. And stout is the one which can get you high. Food There are many platters to choose from. Everything in the menu goes really well with the beer. The place is all about beer after all. The food is proper American style, dont expect it to be spicy. There is an option for fine dining. I would suggest this pub...read more

No comments on food, Good Service, Really good ambiance, Expensive Been here quite a few times and best way to start is to have the sample set. Pick your taste and enjoy. I always order the same items and have a great time at this place. Setting : Friends + Dates

Biere Club is a charming place and has become a landmark in Bangalore in a matter of two years. They are also easily the pioneers of micro-breweries in the city. It's crowded whenever I go; people love the beer here and I find myself a big fan of their wheat beer. They have some decent varieties, and generate some interesting special beers every week. I tried the Belgium Yale once. Smooth and bursting with the kicks! The music is good. They have a different ambiance for every one of their three floors. I really liked their wall art - it's so French! Of course, the second floor is where it's all happening. The best of the city's hip crowd are chilling here with their mugs of beer...read more

POSITIVE rooftop ambience courteous staff fancy crowd Good hangout place for the youth, service is excellent with amazing crowd. I loved the place and the recommended item would be their Platters, definitely recommended :)
Nice place to sit and chat ,, i like 4 cheese pizza there , with wheat beer...

My rating is for the beer, talking positives the brew acts as d magnet though mostly wheat gets over by 9 r half past 9.The smoked chicken pizza joined hands wid my drink. The lady @ the reception is not worth her work. The first tyme I went dere was very disappointd wid gettin a place even stand 2 near the servings. Newaz kuddoz 2 d beer & bookinz must n d onset of weekend.cheerz \m/

I went there for lunch with my brother few months back.. Both of us are not beer fans hence we preferred the 1st floor which is a proper dinning room. We had a mushroom stater which was good. For a cheese lover it's a great dish because it was mushroom baked in cheese.. we ordered the soup of the day which was tomato and basal soup whic was amazing..Main course we picked a chicken dish but found a hair in it. The staff was extremely quick in replacing it infact they were ok with us changing d dish too. Hence ordered a mushroom burger which was amazing...but the best dish was the toffee pudding which was the desert. it just had a great flavor and a really delightful sponge cake. whenever i...read more

The Bier or Beer is by far the best in Bangalore. I have been here twice and tried the Indian Pale Ale and the Belgian Witbier. Both are excellent and considering what one has to pay for a small bottle of Hoegaarden in some of the other places around town, excellent value for money. I am not a beer drinker normally as am not a fan of Indian lager. But having lived in Belgium in the past can really appreciate good beer when I can get it. Can't say much about the food. We only tried some Moroccan Chicken Kebabs which were excellent, just the right amount of spice and tender. Am looking forward to trying more food and beer from their microbreweries.

The Biere Club scores over all the other pubs in Bangalore for location and ambiance. Its near the UB City and attracts the best crowd of Bangalore. Please do reserve your seats prior to your visit else you would be seen arranging for chairs. The environment is bustling and they really play some good music(numbers you would prefer while you drink). Now I dont drink but I can assure you that this place offers "some best beer"(quoting my friends who had drinks). The place is very expensive and hence no prizes for guessing that it attracts a lot of foreigners. Its not a place where you would go for just catching up a drink with your friends. It can be good options for people...read more

The Ultimate place for 'Beer' lovers..i was new to bangalore and when i first visited this place ,it suprised the hell out of me. Starting from the ambience, the food quality and the crowd its a good party place. Not to forget about the beer they serve...awesome every single penny is worth it if u get that kinda taste. If u want to hangout with ur friends ,sit and have a nyc chat while great quality of beer and food is served to you. This is THE PLACE to be. Cheers and happy Drinking..:)

Hav been here searchin for breweries. Had pizza and tasted almost all the varieties (they provide samples) i guess 2nd floor is more happenin than ground floor. Sundays brunch wid fresh beer is well known.. But i will go wid TOIT anyday if compared
I think the biere club is a bit overrated.. Service was disappointing and the food was just mediocre..the beer is fine but you do have a lot of other options in bangalore with their own breweries.
a decent place to be...however the beers are almost same...didn't appreciate much as I felt it was quiet conjusted

Upside - A well designed place..good furniture,nice graphiti etc. The pizzas are great and so is the Beer. Downside - Have been turned down a couple of times for lack of Beer Cocktail. It is "never" available & they may as well strike it off their Menu. The service is slow. Air conditioning is poor in certain areas.
great place to hang out with friends esp. if ur a Beer lover... food is decent... service efficient..really enjoyed it...

Here's what you will go through if you ever visit this place (I suggest you might as well spend your money somewhere else) - 1. Once you go in, if your stars are favoring you, you will manage to grab the attention of the waiters otherwise might as well decide the order and convey the same to the chef / kitchen. 2. This is such a pompous place where the waiters think they are doing a favor by listening to the customer's orders. 3. Also they don't know what menu to display when - when I went here Pizzas / Pastas weren't available but apparently the waiter did not want us to know that. It was only once we asked were we told "hey you village folks this menu ain't available so go take a...read more
Thank you Beer Gods for Bieri Club. No nonsense no frills brew pub that will keep you coming back for more. Love the convenience of this place.

Definitely worth a visit if you love beer. They have a nice variety of beers. Ones of note during our visit were the Belgian Strong Ale, Hefeweizen and Abbey Ale. Both the vegetarian and non-vegetarian platters offer enough variety if not quantity, and are worth it. Unfortunately, the service was painfully slow. Also, the restrooms are inconveniently located on the first floor. The ambiance, while better than the usual fare, could be even better. But on the whole, the great brews and the nice location close to UB city make it a worthwhile place to visit when you're around the area.
It is what it says it is. A good Beer/Brew Club, with inhouse brewery and perfect Ambiance to match various ales. Food menu is a little restrictive, but you don't really go there to eat!

This place is THE place for beer lovers!! This is my first time at an actual brewery... All my life I've just chilled over the regulars,I mean the fosters, Budweisers and not to forget the ubiquitous Kingfishers... Alright so this was a totally new n unique experience for me... The Biere Club looked classy n cool at the same time... From the moment I stepped in I knew it had a great fan following,even though the place was established just about two years back. Classic rock n country music were playing,n these are of course ingredients to the art of chilling in style. The waiters were polite n spoke very good English. This is one of the problems in many of our diners where it's kinda...read more

Bier Lovaah ?? You will enjoy 'Biere Club' to the max :) They brew 8 varieties of beer, including 'mango beer', a seasonal brew. You can try them before you place the order. I've almost tried all the brews, they were good (am not a beer lover by the way). The best time to visit this place is the summer. The Mediterranean food served here is excellent (You will only enjoy it if you are a food-explorer. Not many enjoy this cuisine). The ambiance was fantastic. They play a Very Good Music :) Food: 3.75 / 5 Ambiance: 4 / 5 Service: 3.75 / 5 Looks: 4 / 5 The Feel: 4 / 5 'Love the Bier' 'Leisure" A 'Must Visit' if you are a Food-Explorer.

Biere club is located in the heart of Bangalore..!! This hot spot is one place the party animals would not want to miss..beer served here are mind blowing n the best part is they provide free beer samples ....!! Staffs are pretty friendly Cheers ...
Genuine brews! Still not Brooklyn Lager, but will do (thus spake my snotty self!). Good place to hand around with people with expensive taste. There is a lot to choose from on the menu.

The Biere Club is perfectly located near UB city. I would give a 5 star for the ambience. They had some four varieties of brewed beers and all tasted good. Better to try the beer before ordering - They serve beer in test tube for tasting. The place is quite happening right from the music they play, the wall decors, etc., The prices are bit on the higher side but defintely worth the money. The place is heavily crowded during weekends, so better go early to secure a good spot.

Lived in Bangalore for 3 years, hadn't been to Biere Club - had to remedy that ASAP and remedy that we did. The visit started off on a sour note though, the usual reservation mix up led to us having to wait a good 20 minutes before being seated. Once seated though the evening was off to a great start with the beer flowing free and indigo fm playing lightly on their speakers. The place was crowded and pleasantly boisterous - not too loud, not too quiet - just right! :) Our trio ended up having a pint each of the Wheat, the lager, the ale and the Seasonal. The Wheat and the ale stood out from the rest but i guess this is entirely a matter of personal preference - do experiment and try...read more

Let me begin with the service,sloppy and unorganized. I came here for the hangover breakfast special. The chocolate milkshake was Hershey's syrup with diluted milk and ice. The pancakes were raw and uncooked(uncooked batter stuck on the fork). The portions were small for the fine dine price were paying. The frittatas were bland and the scrambled eggs off mediocre portions. The bread(hot buttered crusty bread) was hard and chewy. The bacon was a little too crispy. Overall,Koshys remains no.1 for a good English/American breakfast.
superb place to try the many varieties of Beer... the food is good too but a little too pricey for my pocket :D... nice crowd and good location.. would be nice to have some offers though..
Never thought beer could get me high till i tried wheat beer at this place

a good place to hang out...located near UB city n with the kinda crowd that comes...it looks very expensive but its actually very reasonable the service is good and so is the ambience the food dere is also good.. its always crowded and they have 2 floors as well overall a good place to catch up with friends

The Place- Nothing much about the ambiance it is pleasant, has 3 floors , cozy is what describes it best. If I have folks in to show around Bangalore Biere Club is on the list, makes me happy we have such spots to hang out in Bangalore. Of course its a perfect gastropub. Good food with brewed beer. Must go there.

They screen all the matches and they brew their own beer. What more can one want? The staff is friendly and the food is good, though everything takes a little longer than it should to come to the table. that could be because they are crowded most of the time. Quite a popular place with the people who lead the UB City kind of lifestyle and like watching matches at pubs.

The wheat beer and the Indian ale(club special) are my favourites.The wheat beer is very smooth,frothy and goes down very well,while the Indian ale is a little bitter and much stronger than the wheat.My strong advice is to follow a pitcher of wheat beer with a pitcher of the Indian ale.Accompany it with a seafood platter.Avoid the steaks on the first level. The place is pricy,but definitely worth it.

Best place if you are fan of wheat beer. Cozy sitting and great theme inside allows spending lazy hours for long. Their food menu is quite short but starters are good. The sitting are is the best part of it. Ambiance - 8/10 Food quality - 8/10 Cost - 7/10 Service - 7/10

Some really good beers! They do a really nice selection of beers from lagers to wheat beers and stouts (light and dark) On the food side they supply very nice platters which come on massive serving plates so you can easily fill yourself up on these platters alone. It as shame there is no lift you have to walk up the stairs to get to the nice bit of the place which is right at the top where you can sit semi outside! All in all, if you want good beer and drinks with a nice atmosphere then this is the place to go in lavelle road area, certainly better than other places in UB city

The biere club! Located in the center of Lavellel road, grabbing all the attention it wants. The place is fine, the wooden texture and the home made beer really sets the mood. Little expensive but its not something you wouldn't be prepared for. The placed serves real good pizzas and pasta. The various beers are also good. The service is little slow. This place is huge, has three floors, the top most is the noisiest one and also offers a smoking zone.

Rats. After enjoying a few brews on the second floor we decided to give the calmer first floor a try. The brew cones are visible here and so were some rats who were enjoying some of the fallen brews in these chambers. On alerting the staff their reactions indicated prior knowledge and the response indicated sheer disregard of the issue. While all of us acknowledge the fact that hygiene is the lowest priority even in the best restaurants in our country we at least expect certain things to remain behind the curtains. Lets hope the establishment here has got the gumption to set new standards in hygiene as it has in brews. Wishful thinking? I hope not! This is my third edit on this...read more

I love the beer club. the club, the atmosphere,ambience and above all the beer. their regular beers are great but we tried the mango beer once which was horrible. the food is also decent. we had pizzas and fries and sandwiches....all of which we enjoyed. however the only problem we faced a couple of times was when we booked they always said they didn't have a booking in our name which is pretty poor if you have booked a table for birthday celebrations. be sure to get the persons name who takes your booking.

The next obvious choice when you watch the Gayle storm live in Chinnaswamy stadium is to head to Biere Club to celebrate RCB's win. Went there with three more people after the match. Found it empty for a change and managed to get a table promptly. Ordered LIIB's for the friends (Long Island Ice Beer). The first set of LIIBs had too much lime in it and did not enjoy it. However, the second round of LIIBs was perfectly done. Also since I don't drink I went for my all time favorite - Hot and Spicy Mary. Ultimate mind boggling wheat beer!! Loved the place the ambience n the wheat beer is damn damn good I'm in love with the beer

Visited this place on 14 April. The music is very loud (& the waiter couldn't assist in getting the volume levels down) and the chairs were uncomfortable. Tried the wheat beer. It was fairly nice. Not too sure if is reasonably priced; but hey for a handcrafted brewery to be located within the city is a blessing & comparable to the ones in other cities. Unlike Delhi & Mumbai where one has to go to Gurgaon & Thane respectively to try out such beers. Compared to Arbor brewing company near MG road, this place lacked in the ambience and variety department.

Loved this place - I forget the name but they had a delicious locally brewed wheat beer that I fell in love with. The guy pouring us the beer was super quick with all sorts of tips and info as well. Too bad they are so far away from where I live - I'd have liked to be back often.
Beers are very tasty and food is decent. However beers may not appeal to all as they are of different flavours and styles that some may not like. Prices are rather high however for what you get.

Being an avid beer lover & having tried different sorts of beer from all over. Biere club is a must visit in bangalore. Service is not superb. However the beer is good, u shud try all. The oven pizzas are to die for

The Biere Club is quite packed for a Saturday evening. The downstairs area is lively and fun for an evening out with friends. The area upstairs is more along the lines of a fine dining area as against the pub area downstairs. Beautifully done, elegant and soft, perfect for a glass of Chardonnay with some buttery mushroom appetizers. Lovely place to unwind and sets quite a romantic mood as well. Must visit.

A great place to eat, drink and have fun with friends , parents , colleagues . It is a huge place and provides you space . The place has more of a corporate feel due to its non-IT youth . The food is simply superb , being a brew pub , the club makes great beer .It is almost equally crowded on every single weekday with a huge crowd on weekends. Overall proves to be one of the very good places to be in Bangalore .

My cousin and I boiled down to this place after an extensive search about where to get drunk on a Friday evening. Been there just twice. Valet parking in that part of town is a necessity so they have taken care of it. Have tried only the wheat beer and I think the nachos. I'll come back with an elaborate review the next time. Weekends around 3-4pm if its raining and you'd like to sit overlooking the road, perfect place to sit back with a beer and friends.!

This place rocks. Amazing beer. All varieties on tap are excellent. Belgian Wit is mind blowing. Dark Ale is one of the best Ales I ever had. Mango beer is refreshing and addictive. My friends had Lager and Stout and praised them a lot. Will try them next time. Ambiance is good. Liked the wall painting. Something different. Food is average but expensive. Service is slow but acceptable.

Bangalore, I'm here to set the record straight. I'll tell you what is up to snuff and what's IN THE ROUGH. The Biere Club is a HOITY TOITY affair opposite MG Road. It's the former place where THE BRASS would meet, and I've never been disappointed by their BREWS. PRESCRIPTION: The pizza is pretty o-kay. Try their dark ALES and their CHICKEN STARTERS. Didnt had the best of experience....bad service , bad food quality and most importantly bad drinks....better luck next time :(

Nice place to chill out with like-minded friends. The variety of beers which they have got is simply overwhelming. Potato wedges here is probably the best I have had. Service is elegant. Never feel like leaving this place once you get in here. The only short-coming is the dozens of policemen who stand right there on Vittal Mallya hunting down the drunk drivers. Sigh.

The last time we were in UB City we figured it was one of those days when one of us wasn't carrying her purse and one of us had just given us a huge treat a week back and so we were kinda low on budget for the Sky Bar kinda outing. The ambience with the crowd is different from the usual places. The brew with the snacks is great. An amazing place for hangout with friends and colleagues…

One of my first night outs in Bangalore and I was but naturally recommended to go to UB city by Bangaloreans. I had been wanting to visit Biere club since a long time having heard about it so much and I got to do that on a friends birthday. It was not a weekend so we did not book our table in advance. Definitely the best pub in bangalore. The wheat beer is a must have...

The best beer breweries in Bang. Love d place and beers. Wheat beer is d best. Stout for hardcore kafi lovers. Starters are best order anything, it will be good. One of the best finds in Bangalore, this lovely microbrewery will provide you with four types of freshly brewed beer (my personal favourite being the the dark ale). Awesome wheat beer n the seasonal oakaged ale was superb.. A place to go, when u need fresh brewed beer in B'lr..

Heafty price for poor food, sometimes unappetising. Service requires some patience. The beers are alright, nothing like Toit (best) or even Arbor Brewing Company for that matter. Live closer to Biere Club but choose to go elsewhere instead (Toit, obviously).

We visited Bangalore in february and we accidentally came across the Biere club, thankfully on our first night in Bangalore and what a find it was. A Great atmosphere, great food and wonderful beer, staff are very efficient and friendly, and the place is 100 per cent clean and hygienic, we were that impressed we are having a stop of in Bangalore on our way to Goa in january just to visit this place, give it a try you will not be disappointed. I was strolling by Lavelle road and suddenly saw this much talked about place... Decided to give it a try as I was keen to visit a microbrewary since moving to Bangalore. Tried 5 different types if fresh brews... Ale, Lager, Wheat, Stout and Seasonal... Interestingly served in 5 test tubes
Excellent place to drink and eat. Good music and excellent weekend crowd

The Biere club visit just makes a day or an evening happy. Prices are a little high but it is worth it. We wanted to try something different and ordered a pitcher of beer with fruit plum. great place to hang out. just perfect brewery with their own beer. and damn good music. just love it.

so it was my first time in Bangalore to a brewery , so we headed to bangaluruz much talked brewery .The Biere Club ..we went on a Saturday evening and greeted by friendly staff, being stayed in Delhi n Mumbai breweries are part of our weekend hang out , especially to taste the water of different varieties.. i enjoyed the wheat beer its stunning , light to have it... Biere Club is a really good hang out and at a real good area might i add. They have beer, beer cocktail and dont worry the non beer folks have their choice as well. The food is also very good. I have only had the chance to try the starters and they are all delicious. i enjoyed the flaming shooter. Wonderful place - both this and the chophouse. Lovely chilled beers, well mixed drinks, right sized portions, delicious food and courteous staff. Would defn reccomend this place for having a great time. Come here atleast twice a month. ----- 27 May, 2013 ----- Great wheat beer.. Must try :) decent food and ambience. Very slow service though

One of the first of it's kind to make it in Bangalore, Biere Club definitely is the best in Bangalore as a hang out, as well as in its authenticity of brewed beers. They have a variety of pitchers for larger groups, and cocktails of beer, as well as samplers (obviously!). They do serve other alcohol, for the non beer folks(!) Usually where there's beer, there's less space for food. Thus, I have not eaten much food here, except the usual fries and wedges, but what I must recommend are the meat balls. They aren't swedish per say, but very tasty and intriguing. I love going to this place, and take my outstation friends and firang people here. Biere Club has set the bar quite high!

Pubbing is vivacious in Bangalore. And locals have a lot of places to choose their watering holes. It is amongst these places that freshly made brews are gaining their identity. The Biere Club on Vithal Mallya road is one such place that stamps the fresh brew concept into your head with a variety of upto 6 fresh brews on tap for you to enjoy. In addition they serve up some mean bites and starter platters that go very well with the drinks on offer. The Biere Club, one the many brewery pubs that are in Bangalore now. The oldest they say. Located in Lavelle road, it is easily noticeable. The place is huge and each floor has its own ambience and appeals different sections of patrons! Like you find the young and energetic crowd on the 2nd floor while a romantic set up in the 1st floor. Though considered to be the premiere brew pub The Biere Club is fast losing out to competitors, and well-deserved it be. Too much of hype has meant indifferent service and discourteous waiters. That in itself is a turn off. Though the ambience is good the choice of beer is limited compared to the other microbrewery options available in Bangalore now. A good enough place to hang out with if you have nothing better to do with friends!

I went to The Biere Club with expectations after reading the positive reviews in Zomato. Unfortunately, I had a very average experience. Me and group of my friends have this penchant to try all the home brewery places in Bangalore. We went to few brewery's here, the Prost, the Arbour brewing company and lately The Toit. The next on the list was this one. Just like other places, we went here and ordered a set of all the home brew beers. Dark and bitter beers are my favorites, i was looking for mind blowing beer here. Like the beer battered fries, which tastes so awesome, must try. They have tried to fry mushroom and stuff with cheese, but it tasted weird! The Biere Club, located in the posh vittal mallaya road and is an excellent example of Bangalore' s pub culture. It is famous for beers and lives up to it's name serving EXCELLENT Home made beers from a brewing machine which will make it in front of you. It has a variety of over 10/12 beers made in house and served chilled directly from tap, old style. Their wheat beer is the best of them all and is a must try having a very distinct taste with a sweetish aftertaste. One unique thing is that they have an option of beer samplers, which is a 60 ml shot glass having a tastikng amount of their beers which will enable you to taste their brews beforehand. I went to this place after checking out the review on zomato. The food was good, the drinks were no less but the ambience was not as i expected. The air conditioning had some problem and to top the hot conditions inside the service was poor. The staff was unresponsive to our requests.

One of the best or probably the Best place in Bangalore to sit down and have a brilliant gulp of Beer! Love the Ale and the wheat Beer! Must admit they have the best beer in Town, Only Toit comes close to this! The place is good! Gives you the old British feel with a touch of contemporary feel attached to it. Service is very SO SO though! You get a feel the Bartenders are the owners of the place and they are serving Alcohol free of cost!! Yikes! The place gets mental on Fridays and Saturdays. Nice place but very over-crowded on Weekends! The lighting is a bit depressing, if your company isn't right.. However, the most cool and chilled-out crowd of bangalore can be found here! I met up with some friends at Beirre Club on a Wednesday night and I was surprised to see that the place was packed, with the ground floor full and the second floor (terrace) was 85% full. Surprisingly though, the ground floor was entirely cleared out by 10:15pm. There were mainly office going people, unwinding with some beer and snacks. There were barely any youngsters around. This is one of the first micro breweries that started in Bangalore and its location on Lavelle Road (centre of the city) attracts people from all over the city. My friends ordered the wheat beer pitcher which they seemed to enjoy because they called for another one as soon as it was over. Their beer mugs are huge...read more

Overview - well the name is well done...intriguing and welcoming to all the biere lovers... !A good place to have a laid back evening with your friends...not exactly the posh and bourgeois ! A very Crowded place on a Saturday evening. I was lucky enough to find a bar stool at the counter. Many people were just standing inside waiting for a table while having their drinks Their Hefeweizen brew was very good. Service was slow (due to the crowd) but food was delicious

After coming to Bangalore I was all excited to explore the city night life.Bangalore was given the status of "party city" back in British times.The question was is it still the same or "Bengaluru" is changed.Me and my friends were staying near to UB city,we started searching nearby clubs to relax after all the hustle bustle during the day,we landed in The Biere Club. I can never forget this place , I throned a party to 25 member group of my friends ..Place was awesome it was made for beer lovers alone , I love the pizza here any day ... Just around the corner on Vittal Malya road, the Biere Club is a good place to hang out with buddies over awesome freshly brewed beer. The thing that stands out is the Beer. I just love their beer, and if I have to compare, I do find it better than Toit. Food is ok, nothing extraordinary. Platters are decent. The ambiance goes with the name of the pub, its really chilled out, nothing sophisticated, just the place to be for Beer. Their seasonal beers are good trys, apart from their classic ale. I have heard complaints from my friends about the service but as per my recent experience, on a busy Saturday night the service was excellent. And I liked the brain hemorrhage shot which was suggested by the tapster and it was really awesome. I will go back at least to have shots :p Beers were really good, food was average, typical bar snacks, nothing worth the price also the portion what they serve is too less.
The microbrewery right in front of UB city, Perfect place to unwind after a hectic day of shopping. The many in house brews is worth trying out and the starters are large in proportions!

If you are a true beer lover, then this is one of those places which you can't miss going to. They usually have 5-6 different types of brewed beers on tap at one point of time. Noons can sometimes be better time to visit the place for a brunch and have a few gulps of freshly brewed beer.

Its a class above.... A worth visiting place to spend some cool time with a chilled Beer. If you are a beer lover then the best place to hangout. Try the Beer sample to taste all the different flavours available. The ambience was excellent with roof top and the service was excellent. Here comes the fooodddd.... A chilled-out place to hang out with your friends. I love the location of the place, and from the first floor, when you look into the street, you feel as if you are sitting in one of those cozy street-corner restaurants in an unknown little town of Europe. A nice place to have very smooth and interesting beers :) .. With pathetic food though ... I had tried stuffed mushroom which came in a very less quantity compared to price we paid .. But the smooth wheat beer made up for it .. Have heard the breakfast there us worth trying . Have not tried yet.

What a crowded place this place is. Always full. You are lucky if you get a place during weekends. Good crowd, good music and good beer. Salads are shockingly good too. Food is really good but a bit pricey. The upper floor is pretty subtle with candlelight and stuff. Good place to chill.

One of the first breweries in Bangalore, this is again an awesome place to visit. Located on Lavelle Road, it has an cool ambiance & decor, with amazing crowd especially over the weekend. A nice place to sit down and relax. Had to wait for 10 mins to get a table. The staff was very friendly and the food service was good. Located in the most upmarket address of the city and right opposite to UB City, it attracts great crowd and manages to rake in good moolah too. The space is huge, and is packed to the brim on weekends. The ambience is nice and is of good quality. The beer is pretty decent, but it can be better. The options it offers are nice and good enough. The let down is the food, the portions are less, the pricing is not apt and the quality is not consistent. It definitely needs to improve on that front. A good place overall, the kind of crowd it attracts and the location is its USP.

Biere Club is one of the first microbreweries in Bangalore. Location is Lavelle Road. Need i say anything more? Its one of the places that you can visit time and again and you will come back for more. This place is very lively and has a old world charm to it. Music is not very high so ideal place to catch up with friends.I like this place more because you can drop by in the middle of the week and grab a mug of the the biere. The place,energy and the charm is a stress buster and it a feel good factor here always! Food is very good and pairs a lot of english and middle eastern flavors. Love this place for what it is and what it has done for the biere lovers!! +1 Photo(s)

I went there just to try a crafted beer. But I ended up ordering a pitcher if Ale beer which mind you was awesome. I had chicken drumsticks with it which was good too. Awesome ambience.. I was enjoying the music too.. Coldplay yellow quite matched up with the mood. Nice experience :)
The Biere (Beer) is awesome... The pizza is awesome, the ambience, location and everything are awesome.

Okay, so my first visit to B'lore as an Adult and out of the innumerable tempting options of pubs and discs finally this is the place where we all ended up. Having said that, I must say the experience was far from disappointing. It was a Saturday Night so the crowd was obvious but I was thrilled to see that every floor of this 3-storied building was bustling with cheerful and jolly middle-aged youngsters joking, laughing loudly, eating and drinking which added a whole 'happy mood' to the place. Though I didn't eat much, the black velvet cocktail and risotto fritters with balsamic mayo distinctly stood out. Fresh Beer Pints were nice and overall had a great time with friends :)

One of the micro-breweries that i actually like because of the variety in food and the amazing brews on the beers. Visited this place during a weekday with some friends. Excellent beer. Loved the Wheat beer. Ale was good too.

This place is simply awesome. Exotic location: nearby UB City and theres a Harley showroom behind. The ambience is so perfect! The food is crazily expensive but does leave you craving for more. Do have the chicken sausage wrapped in bacon. Must visit place if you're in Bangalore.

Much hyped a place..had heard a lot from friends so definitely wanted to visit. However, didn't find it that exciting. The beer was good, found the food ordinary though. Frankly this was my first visit to this Brewery in Bangalore (had been to one in Pune). Its a great place for beers. You have to try one called (not sure if I remember the name correctly) Whisky Beer. It is a superb one. I am not going to reveal how it is made or any of the amazing details. Just go and try it out! For the non-veg lovers, it is filled with some yummy starters that will get you drooling. The ambience is superb. One of the nice place to be on a sunny day. The seating is comfortable and well placed with spaces to walk freely. The food is ok but the rye beer is too good. Do visit this place for a good crowd and good beer.

Biere Club is a fun place to hang out on a weekend or after a long day at work! if you're the kinda person who doesn't like stuffy places, get a seat by the balcony and order that Malibu - it tastes great!!

I liked this pub....good old world vibe, with classic rock playing in the background, friendly banter and chatter all over, giving it a "mondegarish" kind of feel...(mumbaikars would know what I am talking about....) Though a bit congested, its a haven of a place on a -so-expensive location like lavelle road. The drinks are surprisingly quite reasonable, almost unexpected of in Lavelle road. Though the beer is somewhat watery. Loved the potato wedges served, ..nice crisp and hot. Could not say the same about the chicken wings though, they were too sticky to my taste. This is a popular joint, and looks like its popularity is gonna stay!!!

Visited the place with a friend of mine. The place was full even on a weekday. The music was nice, although I found it to be a tag loud. My friend ordered the shandy lager lemonade. It was a beer based cocktail which he really liked even though he is not a beer person. I tried the stout which I really enjoyed, good dark beer. For food we had nachos and potato wedges, good but nothing extraordinary to talk about.

Visited this other microbrewery near UB City today with a friend. Must say, Bangalore has way better places than Delhi NCR! This is an awesome pub which has 3 floors and an excellent ambience. One of the best pubs ...the staff gladly gives you the quirky coasters if you want . best platters . good portions .

One of the best things about this place is it's location. You enter inside and realize the ambience inside is just as good. Having a beer here, was on my wish list before coming to Bangalore, Their in house brewery has made and is making, some good beers and Belgian Wit now is  surely one of it. Their crew  is very courteous in explaining in detail the nature of each custom made beer and this has got the finest ambience and crowd around

My visit to The Biere Club was a long time coming. Finally ended up going with some very special people who came on holiday to visit. Now, i'm not saying that the fact that it was 20 meters away from the new Lamborghini showroom had anything to do with us going there, but i'm sure it deserves to be mentioned! :D The place was pretty crowded, which is why I was quite surprised that the middle floor of the joint was not in service. Considering I had to wait a good 15 minutes for a table. So, these are the kind of problems rich people face, too much business, not enough servers. As with any visit to a micro-brewery, we started off with samplers. None of which my guests liked. And I was...read more

Lavelle road is swamped with fine dining restaurants and raving pubs that just segregates this part of the city from the rest especially on weekends. If you are celebrating a special night, just head to Lavelle road cause that is where the party is. One such ale house around here is The Biere Club, a small shack that is buzzing with crowd and perfect for any date night. The ambiance is versatile, eclectic and subtle all in one. The seating arrangement is more alive at the ground level than at the first floor, so for large crowds book a table in advance. Service is quick and the food rich and tantalizing for your taste buds.The pub has a bit of rustic comfy feeling that can turn any...read more

Awesome place to hang around with your friends. The best thing is the location. The crowd is really nice. This place has a western ambience and the songs they play are really awesome. Make sure you call the official number for booking as I did face some problem regarding reservations. But I did enjoy myself, to be honest!

Had to settle for a table in the top floor on a busy Saturday.. Excellent ambiance with fresh cold breeze.. We ordered the regular Lager, Ale and Stout varieties. In the seasonal variants, we tried out mango. It was decent. At 2345hrs we were asked for the last orders as the place closes by 0030 hrs.. We went for a non veg ham and salami pizza.. It was a wood fired one.. Good taste and salami was on the form of triangular slices on the face of pizza.. Between 2345 and 0030 hrs, we were reminded twice of the deadline and finally at 0030 hrs, we were the last to vacate this place.. Good aspect is the ambiance and the seating arrangements.. Not at all cluttered!!

A nice place to hangout with friends. Good crowd. Interesting beer options. Nice menu too Tried their beer mohito and the one with peach. I did not quite like the peachy beer. However they have many options to choose from or you can go for the regular. The food served is pretty good too.

Do you crave authentic pizza? This has been so far the only place in Bangalore whose pizza transports me back to Milan or Florence. I'm sure the beer is great too but being a work lunch no one had any. The ambiance is nice, sort of a modern hoffbrau house ( German beer hall however it is spelt). The loaded cheesy fries were good but a lil soggy. The cheese with grilled corn and peppers and some jalapenos was nice and the cheese generous. It was missing some creamyness and so I asked for mayo. What came out was a duo of harisa mayo and garlic mayo. Both fantastic and the garlic mayo being thicker and with real roasted garlic went best with this dish. They should bottle and sell their...read more

Went with my friends for this one... Ordered a Kabab platter along with brewed beer.. The chicken Kabab was well done the dip sauce was also perfect for non veg dishes. And the veg had steam cooked potatoes and paneer which was also good.. Over all this place is mainly for beer and for dishes. Coming to the waiters, friendly and explained in detail about the beer and what it contained.. Also have the French fries..

This place has been on my "places to visit in Bangloare list" since too long  hence i was elated to know that this place lived up to everything i've heard about it.This place is situated right next to UB city so its not really difficult to locate it.A big thumbs up to the ambience well as the service there.Though this place lies more on the expensive side,i'd say its still worth every penny.The alcohol here is really nice and i've friends who swear by their awesome beer.The food isnt that great though,we had grilled chicken nachos and it was slightly disappointing.All in all,this place is a must visit for all beer lovers and anyone who justs to chill out with friends
It was one of first kind of to be open in the city. Nice rustic ambiance. They used to give 6 varieties of beer initially to taste complimentary to choose from. Now they have stopped the same

I have known this place since its beginning, however it has sadly evolved to just another place to drink. The food is good but not how it used to be, burnt lamb chops & disproportionate seafood main course don't look very inviting among other small but vital details. Still preferred to be here as the standards are higher. Please watch the food.

If you are a beer lover, this place must be on your list. They have their own brewery which is an added advantage. If you are not sure which beer to order, you can try a sample beer and then decide. Apart from the lager beers you also get ale beers which is relishing. The music is peppy and cheers you up. If you are tired after a week's work and want to rejuvenate along with friends, then this is your place. The crowd is cosmopolitan and makes you feel that you are in England's own backyard. Food is strictly continental but delicious. Best time to visit Saturday evening.

Typical As we were sipping coffee sitting in lavelle road coffee day (LCD) we saw this microbrewery in front of us, one of the very few in Bangalore. And it hit us then that none of us have tried the fresh brewed beer till then! Realizing the insult we've been doing to the beer fraternity we walked across the road to try out. Typical bar setup with long legged wooden chairs and dim lights. They had a very interesting menu with several flavours of beer. U can try a sample beer of 90ml if u r not sure how it would taste. We tried 3 diff flavours and finally sticked to the one with spice flavour. Was good and different. We ordered a veg platter for starters, which was not bad at all....read more

Overall a good experience. The Staff was poor. Waiters never bothered to take orders. We'd to stretch our necks to find one and stare at him hoping he'll look at you at some point of time so u can call him. That said , food was tasty . Nice pizzas . We tried 4 cheese pizza. Crispy squids weren't so great. Same with mashed potatoes.
A lot to yack about....just lets say that the menu is overpriced and the food is no big deal. On the contrary, the manager is good. IF i ever go back it will be because of him.

Ask for the 2nd floor. Nice and classy place. One of my favourite brewery and order the seasonal beer whenever they have it Don't miss the crispy fried cheesy garlic mushrooms Also for the meat lovers, try the wings

In one of the trendiest cities of India, opp the most decadent mall (UB city), lies this club called the bièrre club. So, what is the obvious thing to do? Step in and try the place out. And why would one? You are greeted by a chalkboard that lists the beers that are in the micro brewery, and with the likes of mango, cinnamon flavors, you are bound to walk in. That is, of course, if you are a beer snob. So, the concept of micro breweries had been around for a while, with a lot of them opening up in Bangalore recently. And what makes one stand out from the other is simply the infusions, pricing and the quality. This place has a check in all 3 areas. The pricing is apt, the quality is...read more

Not so much a fan of beer. But I've got to say. Those were the best Pizza's I've ever had in my life. And the sausages as well. In fact. Even I could even tolerate the beerz way better than your Kingfishers

Amazing place. You will just live the beer and the service. Food is pretty good too, i mean the starters. The best and a must try beer is their Octoberfest beer thought its seasonal. The beer just rocks. The sides and starters, nachos, cheese platter are just the right thing to have with the beer. Though i would suggest to stick to beer and not try regular cocktails as a simple mochito did not come up well in taste and flavour. Must go place.

This place is happening...The beer quality is amazing and tastes much better than District 6. This place has quite a good food. ..which is not expected from this place when compared to previous visit. ..The place is quite in center of the city and easily access able. Quality of beers are wonderful...Please ask for flavor of the day and never miss it..at least take a sampler to taste it..
There has always been a crave for tastes to me, be it a beer or food. Now thas has been my fav place to have been brewing wheat beer. Pizzas to platter has been the best combo to me.
Great location - easy to hail a driver for the evening if you have had one too many! good food too, service can be a notch better, but overall a very decent brewery!

I went to this place on weekend with few of my friends. Thats y rush were there. But the crowd was really awesome. Music was awesome. I dont drink liquor thats y i have ordered mock beer & virgin sangrian both were awesome...& as my friends said beer was also good. Ambience is nice. But whenever u plan to go thr ur pocket must be full ;) :D
Nice ambience and music. Went with friends. Staff us helpful and cooperative. Will definitely go again. Lager beer is good. Reservstion is recommended if you are going on weekends evenings.

Ocktober fest is a con job at rs 1500/-unless u can drink till u fall . They claim unlimited beer and sangrias which are diluted with loads of sprite and juice . Food at the ocktoberfest is literally finger food .. fries. And more fries,onion rings. The only food we could really call food were the veg and non veg pizzas. The service was absolutely slow when no of pxs went up. They opened only top floor which could choke u if u r not a smoker cause its their smoking area. The fans were not enough on a hot day like today . Music was too soft had to keep requesting them to increase volume . Quite disappointed .though v stuk around for quite a few hours . I feel you can walk up to...read more

Biere club is the first place in my search for craft beers in bangalore and it did not disappoint. Tried the stout and it was refreshing with heavy taste of coffee and really chilled. It's not even close to a real stout but it's better than drinking kingfisher or haywards. We ordered small fries and the waiter got us large, so there was some confusion. Waiter was rude. So we paid for the beer and left. Where do these restaurants hire people from? All the waiters should be trained in some basic courteous behavior. Not happy with the service but beer is as good as it gets in Bangalore.

THE GREAT BANGALORE MICROBREWERY JOURNEY: THE BIERE CLUB Located on a busy intersection on Lavelle Road, is The Biere Club. I am a great fan of Stand Up Comedy and when I saw that they had Sorabh Pant performing on a Thursday night, my friend and I made a beeline for it. Microbrewery and a comedy show together! What could be better? We were seated in the ground floor section. I really liked the interiors, made me feel like I was in an old English pub. While my craving for a good Irish Stout was left unsatisfied at The Arbor Brewing Company a few days back, Biere Club delivered in grand Fashion. The perfect Irish Stout, very yummy. I followed it up with a unique Orange peel beer....read more
The best brewed beer in town along with amazing Pizza's.

I had heard a lot about this place since d tym i hav shiftd to bangalore bt nevr gota chance to be here..finally came here yestrday...being a sat nyt i expectd d place to b crowded..bt it was kinda empty despite d fact that there is an octoberfest gng on..the seasonal beer was gud..wid an excellent after taste..muat try d biere club non veg platter..d fish is amazing n their meatballs r heavenly..overall it was a gud experience.
Amazing beer. Beer lovers should visit this place. And very nice food and service. I have visited the whitefield outlet. The ambience there is elegant.
An outstanding microbrewery and the beer brewed is really good. There are some spices beers and Indian ales. Food is mainly finger food and ambience of open air is very good.

Having been to most of the micro breweries in town, I can vouch for this place to have quality beer, food and ambience! Keeping up to the hi-fi locality where this pub is located the interiors of this place are well done and make one feel like they are in a pub abroad! All the beers are top notch but my personal favorite would be the irish stout. This stout is like the benchmark for me whenever i order stout elsewhere in the city! The dark color and bitter taste of this beer just cant be matched in town! The food here though expensive is pretty good( dont get the pork sausage platter, total waste of money). Wide variety of starters and mains will give you ample choice to choose from!...read more

This place is distinct and I can see why people would like it; it is energetic and spirited which is cool.This place did not disappoint! It got unbelievably crowded, the management ROCKED. Took really good care of everyone. Can't wait to go back!. we occupied the top floor love the view of the intersection from there . We order their pitcher of "You got be crazy bomb " and just love it ..In food we order four cheeze pizza and it was Delicious , love every single bite of it... This microbrewery is definately a place to visit when you are in Bangalore

Wonderful concept ..... They brew their own beer .... Always full with youngsters .... Loved the cocktails and finger food .... Ambience was cool and the staff extremely courteous ..... A must visit and very exclusive place for all ages
The best micro brewery in the city. Ehatever you order from the menu is the best and you would crave for some more.. the pizzas are to die for.. try it...

I come from a family where drinking is considered a sin. Obviously, i started drinking in college. Once i started interning, me and my friends wanted to go to places which were relatively more expensive. We decided to go the biere club. We had to wait for so long for a table. I don't mind that since it was our mistake. We finally got a table. We ordered soms pasta and shooters. It took like ages to come to the table. Ghey were alright. Then we ordered beer. A pitcher of a lagger that was there in their choices. The waiter actually spilled the beer on my friend and me. If that's not clumsy i dont know what is. Leter it was cleaned and then after sometime the waitors asked us to vacate the...read more

If you are feeling fancy, and fancy having a tall glass of good beer or ale then you should drop by this place and make sure one of you made reservations. These guys brew their own beer and also provide a good atmosphere for you to enjoy it. So now that you are here you must try the "You got to be crazy bomb".I am assuming you have already finished your glass of ale or wheat beer or club special.If not then why are you here??what are you doing with your life??Where is my beer?? You will get to the second question only if you had a bad day and got drunk after you answered the first question as "for beer"(don't worry the good vibes and alcohol help ignore the second...read more

Have been frequenting the place for a while. While the location is to die for, given the fact that there are multiple micro breweries in town now, it's high time the biere club management takes a serious look at the competition and buck up. Service needs to improve, menu needs a reboot & appoint few good managers as well. Tapping on the regular customers would certainly help sustain. Wishing the management all the very best.

Been hearing about this place for a while. Finally got a chance to come here. Love the beer and the ambience! Service was also pretty good. But food was ice cold by the time it came to the table. It's an ideal place for brunch.

The Biere Club has the best beer in this city. That's why I keep going back to this place. All of their beers are of great quality, but my favourites are the Irish Stout, Belgian Ale and Wheat Beer. Even the seasonal beer here has an element of surprise - mango beer and orange peel beer. In a place like this, food becomes secondary but I've tried a few things and have never been disappointed by the quality of the food served. Chicken wings are great and so is the special platters and pizza. The ambiance reminds you of a European pub and the music gets you in the mood to enjoy the amazing crafted beers. If you are lucky, you can catch a live performance with your beer. The staff knows...read more
Great place, very good beers and good food as well. The tasters menu is good if you can't decide which beer is for you but the wheat beer is probably the best there..good service as well.

Situated in the better part of Bangalore this restro pub dishes out a variety of beers which i never thought existed. Its a micro brewery basically and they make their own beer. I tried out wheat beer and coffee beer, called stout. The pitchers are a bit pricy at 800, but who is complaining. Tried out onion rings also which are made after soaking the onions in beer. Thin crust pizza with mushroom topping was ok, anyways who concentrates on food when you get such nice beers. Ambiance is like a pub should be, but i liked the wooden stairs and flooring. Overall its a place where you can go frequently and I'm so waiting for my next visit. P.S. they are open in Whitefield also.
A nice brewery with a pretty good menu. The platters are awesome. It's not very pocket friendly but each penny is worth spending.

One of the best breweries in the town. I had been there for the Okotberfest and felt it was worth every penny. The beers are definitely the best i have had in bangalore. The food was also pretty good although i didnt try much apart from the pizzas, onion rings and fries. The service is pretty good but it may slow down during the peak hours. Overall, it was really good and I recommend all the beer lovers to visit it.

Been here few times and it always proved a great place to hang out with friends. Their micro brewery is average compared to others. But the food here is very good. We ordered nachos n salad. Very good. Main course they have pizzas n burgers. Veg pizza was real good. Overall Ambience: 4/5 Service: 4/5 Food: 4.5 /5 Cost: 4/5
The liveliness of this place won my heart!!!! Had been to this place last week with my friends it was really fun. Food and drinks and the variety will make u go crazyyy.
